title: Electroporation Protocol

For control electroporation dilute pUC19 to 10 pg/µl with Milli-Q water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"535t5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c8kim","text":"Calculation:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c1kl6","text":"If the culture was diluted 1000-fold when plated, the total cfu per ml is 1000 times the number of colonies counted. The cfu is divided by the amount of pUC19 (10 pg per ml)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6jp19","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7qe2c","text":"cfu/ µg = (colonies counted*1000) / (0.00001 µg pUC19)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":"","6_0":"","6_1":""},"colWidths":[null,null],"data":[["Antibiotic","Working Concentration"],["Ampicillin","100 µg/ml"],["Carbenicillin","100 µg/ml"],["Chloramphenicol","33 µg/ml"],["Kanamycin","30 µg/ml"],["Streptomycin","25 µg/ml"],["Tetracycline","15 µg/ml"]],"guid":"95E31080457F11E9A24D69A8AA1320D3","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[],"show_headers":true}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1j3gg","text":"For control electroporation dilute pUC19 to 10 pg/µl with Milli-Q water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bodpq","text":"The electroporation protocol will vary depending on the strain so this protocol may need to be optimized.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Turn on electroporator and set to 1.7-2.5 kv (optimize for strain), 200 ohms and 25 µF
Place recovery SOC in 37°C water bath
Thaw cells on ice for 10 min or use freshly made cells 00:10:00
Place appropriate number of microcentrifuge tubes and 1 mm-electroporation cuvettes on ice
Flick the tube containing cells a few times to mix and add 25 µl of competent cells to the microcentrifuge tube undetermined
Add 1 µl of a 100 pg/µl to 1 ng/µl DNA solution (in DI water) to the cells in the microcentrifuge tube undetermined
Transfer the DNA-cell mixture to the cold cuvette, tap on countertop 2X, wipe water from exterior of cuvette and place in the electroporation module and press pulse ( you don’t hold the button down )
Immediately add 975 µl of 37°C SOC, mix by pipetting up and down once and transfer to a microcentrifuge tube, 5 ml culture tube, or 15 ml centrifuge tube. undetermined
Place in the shaker/incubator at 37°C incubator for 1 h 01:00:00

Make appropriate dilutions When using 100 pg - 1 ng of DNA, make three dilutions: Dilute 1 µl of cells into 990 µl SOC and plate 100 µl. (10000-fold dilution) Dilute 10 µl of cells into 990 µl SOC and plate 100 µl. (1000-fold dilution) Dilute 100 µl of cells into 900 µl SOC and plate 100 µl. (100-fold dilution)
creator: Ken Christensen
